note: seq=45 (PGDOOR LISTEN-FIX, lifeline-grade, PG-SAFE). The seq-44 audit proved seq-43 set the scoped pg_hba line + UFW rule correctly but PG stayed bound to localhost (listen_addresses=localhost; ss shows 127.0.0.1/[::1] only) -- the main-config edit was overridden. This runs /opt/atlas/pg_listen_fix.py which uses `ALTER SYSTEM SET listen_addresses='*'` (written to postgresql.auto.conf, which Postgres loads LAST and which overrides every other config file), then a GUARDED `systemctl restart postgresql` with a pg_isready readiness loop + AUTO-RESTORE: if PG is not ready it runs `ALTER SYSTEM RESET listen_addresses`, restarts until PG is back UP, and fails loud (PG alive, nothing widened). On success it re-confirms via ss + pg_isready and REPUBLISHES status/hetzner/pgdoor-audit.json. NOTE: the box's own pg_isready -h <public-ip> can read false even when correctly bound, because UFW scopes 5432 to 64.20.50.3 only (the box's own source IP isn't the peer); the definitive box-side proof is external_bind=true (ss shows 0.0.0.0:5432) + scoped pg_hba + scoped UFW, with the final 5432 handshake coming from 64.20.50.3 via the InterServer one-shot. NO stop/disable; only ALTER SYSTEM + restart, guarded. Idempotent (noop if already external). HONEST: not run against live PG from the sandbox; py_compile clean + --selftest PASS (ss external-bind detector). Allowlist-safe (cmds start with /opt/atlas/).

steps.note: seq=45: ALTER SYSTEM listen_addresses='*' + guarded restart + re-audit. Auto-restore on failure.

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; w={'/opt/atlas/pg_listen_fix.py':'731b285626c1a48b0bf727845a7fc7c5d46b50d07d756d197475e11fdda1138e'}; [sys.exit('SHA MISMATCH '+p) for p,h in w.items() if hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest()!=h]; print('sha ok')"
